_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, Northern Virginia (Fairfax/Prince William) districts 17-21 (state tier). McGuire (HD-17,D), Tran (HD-18,D), Henson (HD-19,D), Maldonado (HD-20,D), Thomas (HD-21,D). Evidence-first finalization from co-patronage records, interest-group scorecards (REPRO Rising, Giffords, Victory Fund), and on-record public statements/legislation. Individual roll-call lines on the HJ1/HJ9 constitutional amendments could not be pulled from VPAP/LIS (403/JS-blocked) this run, so amendment FALSEs are anchored to documented co-patronage, A+ REPRO grades, and the legislators' own public advocacy rather than asserted roll-call reads. CAVEATS: (1) McGuire was seated 2026-01-21, AFTER the Jan-14 amendment votes, so those are NOT imputed to him; his record is thin (2 chief-patroned 2026 gun-control laws + a pro-choice endorsement). (2) Maldonado RESIGNED effective 2026-05-31 to care for family -> marked status:former; she carries a documented deviation (declined NARAL's endorsement over its police-defunding stance) scored as a public_justice credit. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; no-evidence cells left null.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — no public statement of faith or church affiliation located.
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-17 (Fairfax County — Franconia/Kingstowne/Springfield). Won the Jan 20, 2026 special general election (~80%) to fill the vacancy left by Mark Sickles (resigned to become Sec. of Finance); sworn in Jan 21, 2026. Virginia Tech (BA + MPA); AT&T Regional Director for External & Legislative Affairs; former chair of the Fairfax-Falls Church Community Services Board.

garrett-mcguire.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-17 (Fairfax Co.); seated Jan 21, 2026 via special election. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] VERY THIN RECORD — only the partial 2026 session. CRITICAL TIMING: the marriage (HJ3) and reproductive-freedom (HJ1) constitutional-amendment votes both passed the House Jan 14, 2026, ONE WEEK BEFORE McGuire was seated, so neither is imputed to him. Documented HARD record: chief patron of two gun-control laws signed by Gov. Spanberger and enacted Apr 23, 2026 — HB1524 (bars carrying 'assault firearms'/>15-round magazines in public areas) and HB1525 (bars handgun/assault-firearm purchase under 21); NRA-ILA condemned both. SOFT: REPRO Rising VA PAC endorsement + his own statement pledging to 'protect reproductive freedom' (sanctity contrary). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on marriage, family/child, Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, election integrity — left null, not inferred from party.

0.note: Endorsed by REPRO Rising VA PAC; own statement pledging to 'protect reproductive freedom in Virginia' and maintain abortion/contraception access — does not affirm personhood from conception. (Endorsement + public statement; not a roll-call — he was not yet seated for HJ1.)

1.note: Chief patron of HB1524 (enacted Apr 23, 2026), which bars carrying 'assault firearms' and >15-round-magazine semiauto rifles/pistols in public areas — an assault-weapon/magazine restriction; NRA-ILA condemned it as unconstitutional.

4.note: Chief patron of HB1525 (enacted Apr 23, 2026) barring handgun/assault-firearm purchase by anyone under 21 — a targeted citizen-disarmament initiative.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — no public statement of faith located.
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-18 (Fairfax County, Springfield/Franconia); represented old HD-42 2018-2024. House Democratic Caucus Chair (since Nov 2023). Born 1978 in Vietnam, came to the U.S. as an infant refugee; Duke BA, Univ. of Michigan MSW; former U.S. Dept. of Labor and National Immigration Forum. First Vietnamese American elected to Virginia state government. Re-elected Nov 2025 (~72%).

kathy-tran.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-18 (Fairfax Co.); House Democratic Caucus Chair; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Deep progressive HARD record and a caucus LEADER on the measures most opposed by this rubric. Sanctity: CHIEF PATRON of HB2491 (2019 'Repeal Act' removing third-trimester abortion safeguards — she testified it had no gestational limit; PolitiFact rated a related claim False) and public champion (as Caucus Chair) of the HJ1 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment. Marriage: as Caucus Chair publicly celebrated passage of the HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment; patron of HB1015 treating gender identity/sexual orientation as protected classes. Immigration: CHIEF PATRON of HB1211 (2020) granting driver's licenses to undocumented immigrants; associated with 2026 bills restricting state/local ICE cooperation. 2A: Giffords PAC + Brady-endorsed; patron of HB217 (assault-firearm/large-capacity-magazine restriction). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on family/child sovereignty specifics, Christian liberty, federal overreach, or election-integrity mechanics — left null. (Individual amendment roll-calls not pulled this run — VPAP/LIS blocked; FALSEs anchored to chief-patronage + on-record caucus advocacy.)

0.note: Chief patron of HB2491 (2019), which removed third-trimester abortion safeguards; testified the bill had no gestational limit — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: As Caucus Chair publicly championed the HJ1 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: As House Democratic Caucus Chair publicly celebrated passage of the HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Champion of codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Patron of HB1015 treating gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes — affirms gender-identity framing rather than immutable biological sex.

Associated with 2026 legislation restricting state/local cooperation with federal ICE enforcement (sanctuary-aligned); chief patron of HB1211 (2020) granting driver's licenses to undocumented immigrants — opposite of opposing sanctuary policy.

1.note: Patron of HB217 (assault-firearm / large-capacity-magazine restriction); endorsed by Giffords PAC and Brady — opposite of opposing assault-weapon/magazine bans.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — no publicly stated faith or denomination located.
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-19 (Prince William/Fairfax — Woodbridge area); assumed office Jan 10, 2024; re-elected Nov 2025 (effectively unopposed). First openly gay Black man elected to the Virginia General Assembly; Vice Chair of the LGBTQ+ Legislative Caucus and a House Democratic Deputy Whip. Virginia State University (BA); UMGC (MBA); federal contractor/program manager; founded a Moms Demand Action chapter.

rozia-henson.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-19 (Prince William/Fairfax); re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Sanctity: co-patron of HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment; REPRO Rising 'Champion.' Marriage: chief co-patron of HJ9 same-sex-marriage amendment; openly gay; LGBTQ+ Victory Fund endorsed; on record pledging Democrats 'will protect trans kids' in schools and criticizing GOP 'gender ideology.' 2A: Giffords 'Gun Sense Candidate,' founded a Moms Demand Action chapter; backs banning ghost/3D-printed guns and disarming domestic abusers. Economic record (min wage to $15, repeal right-to-work, collective-bargaining expansion, overtime for domestic workers) and HB26 marijuana resentencing did not map to any state-tier scoreable cell -> left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, election-integrity mechanics. (Amendment roll-calls not pulled this run; FALSEs anchored to co-patronage + endorsements + public statements.)

0.note: Co-patron of HJ1 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; named a REPRO Rising 'Reproductive Freedom Champion' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: REPRO Rising Champion who co-patroned the abortion-rights amendment — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Chief co-patron of HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment; openly gay; LGBTQ+ Victory Fund endorsed — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Co-patron of the amendment codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Pledged Democrats 'will protect trans kids' and criticized GOP 'gender ideology' efforts — affirms transgender framing rather than immutable biological sex.

4.note: Publicly supports protecting transgender identity in public schools — promotion of LGBTQ identity in school policy.

1.note: Giffords PAC 'Gun Sense Candidate'; founded a Moms Demand Action chapter; backs banning ghost/3D-printed firearms and disarming domestic abusers — opposite of opposing gun bans/registries.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a self-stated denomination (Cape Verdean heritage is predominantly Roman Catholic, but that is heritage context, not a confirmed personal affiliation — not scored).
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-20 (Prince William — Manassas area); represented old HD-50 2022-2024. RESIGNED effective May 31, 2026 to care for terminally-ill/ailing parents; special election pending. Daughter of Cape Verdean immigrants; Barnard BA, GWU JD; technology attorney and small-business founder; chaired a Courts of Justice judicial subcommittee and the Dem Caucus Affordable Housing Policy Committee.

michelle-maldonado.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-20 (Prince William Co.) — RESIGNED effective 2026-05-31 to care for family (marked status:former). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record with one notable conservative-coded deviation. Sanctity: REPRO Rising 2025 'A+ Champion'; Pro-Choice on Choice Tracker. Marriage: public supporter of the HJ9 same-sex-marriage amendment (own Q&A says she 'helped pass' marriage equality; spoke at a marriage-equality gala). Family: skeptical of school-choice vouchers, strong teachers'-union (PWEA) ally. 2A: Giffords-endorsed; co-patroned gun-storage/firearm-lock bills and a firearm-violence-prevention center. NOTABLE DEVIATION (scored as credit): publicly DECLINED NARAL Pro-Choice America's endorsement specifically because of NARAL's police-defunding stance — a documented public stance against defunding -> public_justice credit. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration specifics, election-integrity mechanics. (Amendment roll-calls not pulled this run; FALSEs anchored to A+ scorecard grade, co-patronage, and her own public statements.)

0.note: REPRO Rising 2025 'A+ Champion' (highest tier — voted the full reproductive-freedom slate incl. the HJ1 amendment); listed Pro-Choice on Choice Tracker — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: A+ abortion-rights champion — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Own campaign Q&A lists 'marriage equality' among the amendments she 'helped pass'; spoke at a Virginia marriage-equality gala — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Public supporter of the HJ9 amendment codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

0.note: Skeptical of school-choice vouchers; strong ally of the Prince William Education Association (teachers' union); centers public-school funding — opposite of supporting universal school choice.

Publicly declined NARAL Pro-Choice America's endorsement specifically because of NARAL's police-defunding stance — a documented public stance against defunding/dismantling police (heterodox credit, not party-line).

1.note: Giffords-endorsed; co-patroned bills strengthening gun-storage requirements and expanding firearm-lock access, and helped create the VA Center for Firearm Violence Intervention — opposite of opposing gun restrictions.

profile.religion: Listed as Episcopal on a Ballotpedia candidate survey (SOFT — not independently re-verified; no direct statement of faith on his own channels).
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-21 (Prince William — Woodbridge/Lake Ridge/Occoquan); assumed office Jan 10, 2024; re-elected Nov 2025 (~58%). U.S. Marine Corps veteran (Afghanistan/Middle East tours); commercial real-estate attorney focused on housing; Vanderbilt BA, William & Mary JD. Committees: Courts of Justice, General Laws, Public Safety.

josh-thomas.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-21 (Prince William Co.); Marine veteran and housing attorney; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive record on the rubric's culture-war axes. Sanctity: REPRO Rising 2025 'A+' Champion; own site pledges to 'fight against any attempts to restrict women's access to reproductive healthcare.' Marriage: co-patron of HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment. 2A: Giffords-endorsed; own site backs background checks, cool-off periods, and red-flag laws. Sponsored bills HB155 (data-center ratepayer protection — tabled), HB2071 (incarcerated-veterans reentry — passed), HB1626 (teacher non-academic-training cap — passed) did not map to scoreable state-tier cells -> left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on family/child specifics, Christian liberty (the HJ9 clergy-conscience clause is boilerplate, not his authorship), federal overreach, immigration, election integrity. (Amendment roll-calls not pulled this run; FALSEs anchored to A+ grade, co-patronage, endorsement, and his own stated positions.)

0.note: REPRO Rising 2025 'A+' Champion; own campaign site pledges to 'fight against any attempts to restrict women's access to reproductive healthcare' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: A+ abortion-rights champion — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ9 (2025), the constitutional amendment to repeal Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban and establish an affirmative right to marry regardless of sex/gender — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Co-patron of the amendment codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
Giffords PAC endorsed; own site backs background checks, cool-off periods, and red-flag laws — opposite of opposing red-flag laws.
